LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Continued To Feb-19-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Based On Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Nov-06-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Garner, Esq. 916-573-3926 of Garner & Associates for Plaintiff.
Erika Gaspar, Esq. 916-749-0278 for Plaintiff.
J. Barrett Marum, Esq.650-815-2600 of Sheppard Mullin for Defendant TIMOTHY SLOAN, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant Argues the Court can deviate/make an exception. Plaintiff responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT TIMOTHY SLOAN, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Stay Based On Forum Non Conveniens.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants' motion to stay based on forum non conveniens is denied. "It is black letter law that, when a federal action has been filed covering the same subject matter as is involved in a California action, the California court has the discretion but not the obligation to stay the state court action." (Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 804; see also Mave Enterprises, Inc. v. Travelers Indemnity Co. (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1408, 1423 ["In general, 'the pendency of a prior action in a court of competent jurisdiction, predicated on the same cause of action and between the same parties, constitutes good ground for abatement of a later action within the same jurisdiction either ion the same court or in another court having . . . jurisdiction'"].) However, the parties to this action are not "the same parties" as the parties to the Eastern District of California action. Mr. Sloan is not and cannot be made a party to that action, and thus the Eastern District is not a suitable alternative forum. Thus, a stay is not available under the forum non conveniens doctrine. (See Stangvik v. Shiley (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 751 ["In determining whether to grant a motion based on forum non conveniens, a court must first determine whether the alternate forum is a 'suitable' place for trial. If it is, the next step is to consider the private interests of the litigants and the interests of the public in retaining the action for trial in California . . . The action will not be dismissed unless a suitable alternative forum is available to the plaintiff."]; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assur. Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431, 433, 439-440 ["a defendant seeking to stay or dismiss an action on the basis of forum non conveniens must show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction in the proposed alternative forum."].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
